titleStmt.title: Despatch from Owen L. W. Smith to David J. Hill, November 27, 1900

distributor.name: Center for Digital Research in the Humanities

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported License. Derivatives must be credited to To Enter Africa from America, made available non-commercially, and distributed under the same terms. Requests for permission for commercial publication or other use should be emailed to the project team.

bibl.title: Despatch from Owen L. W. Smith to David J. Hill, November 27, 1900
Despatches from United States Consuls in Monrovia, 1852-1906

correspAction.roleName: U.S. Minister Resident and Consul General

div1.p:
No. 55, Consular.
Legation of the United States,
Monrovia Liberia;
Nov. 27, 1900.

Hon. David J. Hill,
Assistant Secretary of State,
Washington.
William Parker, late Spokane, Washington, dead.
William Parker, late of Spokane, State of Washington, reached Monrovia, near Sept. 1, 1900, Left for Robertsport, (Cape Mount) Liberia, Sept. 27, 1900, and died at Robertsport, Nov. 13, 1900.

No. 55. Consular,
Legation of the United States, Monrovia Liberia; Nov. 27, 1900
Hon. David J. Hill, Assistant Secretary of State, Washington.

I have the honor to transmit to you the intelligence, that William Parker, late of Spokane, State of Washington, who reached Monrovia, on or about Sept. 1, 1900, and left here Sept. 27, 1900, for Robertsport, (Cape Mount) Liberia; died at Robertsport, Nov. 13, 1900. His widow, Mrs. Emma Parker, in still in the United States, and resides at No. 325, First Ave. South; Minneapolis, Minn. I
I have not notified the widow.

I have the honor to be
Your obedient servant;
Owen L. W. Smith,
M. R. and C. General.
